# RustyNES v2.0.1 — "Harbor"

**The first release of the v2.0.x "Harbor" mobile-finalization train.** With the
breaking v2.0.0 "Timebase" scheduler rewrite shipped, Harbor opens the runway to
the joint mobile store launch (v2.1.0): the Android app is **re-ported onto the
one-clock v2.0.0 core**, the **AccuracyCoin oracle is re-synced to upstream**, and
the housekeeping that v2.0.0 (#225) deferred to "v2.0.1+" is done. Every core
change is **behaviour-neutral** — the deterministic chip stack is byte-identical to
v2.0.0, `nestest` stays 0-diff, and AccuracyCoin holds its honest **139/141
(98.58%)**. No store submission yet (that is v2.1.0); Android continues as
GitHub-sideload.

---

## Android re-ported onto Timebase (#235)

The `rustynes-android` host + Jetpack Compose app rebuild against the one-clock
`rustynes-mobile` UniFFI bridge (regenerated against **uniffi 0.32**);
`versionCode 20000 → 20001`, `versionName "2.0.0" → "2.0.1"`. The deterministic
chip stack is untouched, so on-device accuracy tracks the desktop core exactly.

The **`.rnm` movie-epoch warning** now reaches the mobile bridge (ADR 0028):
`NesController::movie_play` peeks `rustynes_core::recorded_before_v2_timebase` and,
for a pre-v2.0.0 recording, queues a drainable host warning (new
`drain_warnings`) — parity with the desktop + wasm frontends. Input replay still
proceeds; the host can now tell the user that exact framebuffer/audio reproduction
is not guaranteed across the engine-timebase boundary, rather than silently
implying byte-exactness.

## The `foss` / `play` Android flavor split — structural start (ADR 0025, #235)

Harbor lays the groundwork for shipping to **F-Droid + GitHub sideload** (clean,
Google-free, ad-free) *and* **Google Play** from one codebase. A new
`distribution` flavor dimension adds a default **`foss`** flavor and a **`play`**
flavor; the five proprietary Google-Play subsystems — Play Billing, the Cast
Application Framework, Play Games v2, Play Integrity, in-app update/review — move
from unconditional `implementation(…)` to **`playImplementation(…)`**, so the
`foss` variant links **none** of them. Their glue lives behind `src/main` façades
with **no-op `src/foss/`** twins and real `src/play/` implementations (including
the CAF `MediaRouteButton`, routed through the `ChromecastSender` façade so
`src/main` links no `androidx.mediarouter` / `com.google.android.gms.cast`). CI
now builds **both** `assembleFoss*` and `assemblePlay*`. Monetization stays
dormant; on-device flavor verification is deferred to v2.0.9.

## AccuracyCoin oracle re-synced to upstream (#232)

The vendored `100thCoin/AccuracyCoin` battery is re-synced: the catalog grows
**144 → 146 rows / 139 → 141 assigned tests**, adding the two new upstream PPU
tests **"ALE + Read"** and **"Hybrid Addresses"** (plus the upstream "$2002 Flag
Timing" + "Sprites On Scanline 0" fixes folded into existing rows). The drift-guard
moves to `catalog().len() == 146`.

**Measured honestly: 139/141 (98.58%).** The two new tests are known gaps — they
exercise the deep sub-instruction PPU octal-latch address-corruption timing when
`$2007` is touched mid-render. Two bounded fixes were attempted this release —
Option 2 (`mc-ppu-bus-addr-hybrid`, Mesen2's persistent `_ppuBusAddress` model,
PR #234) and Option 1 (a 2-cycle-ALE fetch refactor, draft PR #236) — and **both
reach only 139/141 with the flag on**, empirically confirming (**ADR 0030**) that
closing these needs a dedicated Timebase-scale campaign, not a bounded fork. Both
ship **default-off**; the shipped build is byte-identical at 139/141. The full fix
is the headline accuracy work of the v2.0.2–v2.0.4 cycle.

## Housekeeping (#237)

The dead **`mc-r1-dmc-abort-probe`** diagnostic feature flag — a default-off
DMC-DMA-abort scheduling probe left over from the v2.0.0 R-1 work — is removed in
full (3 `[features]` decls, the ~210-line gated `abort_probe` module + its 13
`#[cfg]` sites, the `bus.rs` log hooks). It gated **only** diagnostic counters,
never shipped behaviour, so the default build is byte-identical. The
`mmc3-m2-phase-irq` experiment was evaluated for the same treatment and
deliberately **kept** — it gates real alternate MMC3-IRQ-timing behaviour and
remains tied to the open R1/R2 residual (ADR 0002). A **measure-first perf pass**
(`full_frame` re-measured at 3.77 ms / 4.42× realtime) adopted nothing per the
>3%-Criterion + byte-identical bar.

## CI cost/time optimization (#233)

The heavy CI legs (`test-roms`, `bench`, the full macOS/Windows matrix) now run
only on `release/*` PRs plus a weekly drift-net cron — not on every intermediate
PR — while the required correctness gates still run everywhere. Intermediate
review cycles are dramatically cheaper; a release branch (like this one) still
gets the full battery.

## Dependencies

**uniffi 0.31 → 0.32**, **mlua 0.11 → 0.12**, **wgpu/naga 29.0.3 → 29.0.4**,
**cc 1.2.65 → 1.2.66**; security-advisory surface re-verified. Consolidates and
closes Dependabot #226–#230. **wgpu/naga 30.0.0 is deferred**: `egui-wgpu 0.35`
pins `wgpu ^29` and egui 0.35 is the newest release, so the majors cannot resolve
until an egui 0.36+ targets wgpu 30.

---

## What's next — the rest of "Harbor"

- **v2.0.2–v2.0.4** — Android feature-parity finalization, foss/play on-device
  verification + monetization wiring, and the **full ALE+Read / Hybrid Addresses
  accuracy fix** (Option 1 to 141/141).
- **v2.0.5–v2.0.8** — iOS finalization on the v2.0.0 core.
- **v2.0.9** — joint readiness verification for both apps.
- **v2.1.0** — the **joint Google Play + Apple App Store + AltStore PAL + F-Droid
  launch**.

Full detail: `CHANGELOG.md` `[2.0.1]` · `docs/STATUS.md` (single source of truth) ·
`to-dos/plans/v2.0.1-harbor-plan.md`.
